scan-and-fix.prompt: Scan repos for open issues (bugs, feature requests, enhancements -- all types, not just bugs). First, discover new repos by running `find /q/src -maxdepth 4 -name ".git" -type d` and comparing against the known target repos table. For any new repo found: check if it has uncommitted changes on non-default branches (skip those); if clean, run `git pull` to sync. Then for each known target repo, pick the highest-priority unfixed issue, analyze the root cause, implement a fix, and submit a PR. In the scan report, add a 'New Repos Detected' section listing any new repos with their path and a copyable CLI suggestion to add them. Follow the instructions in your agent file.
scan-and-fix.description: Scans repos for all open issues (bugs, enhancements, feature requests), discovers new repos, analyzes root cause, creates fix PRs

open-pr-maintenance.prompt:
Maintain all open PRs I authored across target repos. For each repo: (1) List my open PRs with `gh pr list --author @me --state open`. (2) For each PR, check for merge conflicts -- if found, rebase or merge main, resolve conflicts preserving intent, push updated branch, comment that conflicts were resolved. (3) Check for unresolved review comments -- identify comments requesting changes or asking questions with no reply from me. For each: read the code, implement the requested change or answer the question, push fix, reply to the comment. Skip PRs with no conflicts and no unaddressed comments. Follow safety rules in your agent file. Include the standard VO subtitle in any HTML report:

Agent: Bug Killer | Job: open-pr-maintenance | Start: | Complete:
open-pr-maintenance.description: Maintain open PRs: resolve merge conflicts and address review comments

daily-summary.prompt:
Generate a daily summary report for all bug-killer activity from the past 24 hours. Steps: (1) List all HTML files in Q:/src/personal_projects/virtual-office/output/bug-killer/ modified in the last 24 hours (exclude *-latest.html symlinks and the daily-summary files themselves). (2) For each file, extract from the HTML: the issue/PR number, repo name, title, outcome (fixed/skipped/failed), and the PR URL if one was created. (3) Also collect and link reports from ALL other bug-killer jobs (scan-and-fix, open-pr-maintenance) that ran in the past 24h -- the daily summary should be the single report to keep open, linking to all individual reports. Do NOT open individual reports in Edge. (4) Generate a self-contained HTML report saved to Q:/src/personal_projects/virtual-office/output/bug-killer/daily-summary-YYYYMMDD.html and also copy as daily-summary-latest.html. The report must: use the same dark theme as existing bug-killer reports (background #1a1a2e, accent #00d4ff), include a standard VO subtitle immediately below the main title:

Agent: Bug Killer | Job: daily-summary | Start: | Complete:

, show a summary card with counts (issues fixed, PRs created, PR comments addressed, merge conflicts resolved, skipped/no-op), and then a table where each row links to the individual run report as a file:/// URL (e.g. file:///Q:/src/personal_projects/virtual-office/output/bug-killer/scan-20260323-100445.html). Columns: Time, Job Type (scan-and-fix / open-pr-maintenance), Repo, Issue/PR, Title, Outcome, Link to Report. Do NOT duplicate or summarize the content of individual reports -- only link to them. If no activity in the past 24h, generate the report anyway showing 'No activity in the past 24 hours.' At the end of the report, add an Agent Job Run Durations section by running: python Q:/src/personal_projects/virtual-office/runner/Get-JobDurations.py --agent bug-killer. Insert the bar chart and table HTML output into the report using the same card/styling as the rest of the report. (5) Open ONLY the daily-summary report in Edge using a tab group: Start-Process msedge '--new-window --group-name=Daily ""'
daily-summary.description: Daily summary linking all bug-killer activity reports from the past 24h, opened in Edge Daily tab group
